The bill has been passed in both houses.

Though it usually isn't a cause for concern, if it happens often, it could reveal deeper health issues. Adopting measures like rising gradually and ensuring you drink enough fluids can enhance your cardiovascular wellness and stability.

Zerodha founder Nithin Kamath says I don't use internet banking apps; here's why
description: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath revealed he avoids banking apps due to their invasive permissions, questioning why they need access to SMS, contacts, and calls. He highlighted the Principle of Least Privilege, stating apps should only access necessary data. In contrast, Zerodha's Kite app requests zero permissions, a deliberate choice reflecting their 'do unto others' philosophy, fostering user trust.

title: Why do sloths move so slowly?
description: If a sloth replaced the tortoise in Aesop’s fable of The Tortoise and the Hare, how long would the hare need to sleep for the sloth to win the race?

title: US-Iran war: How the LPG cylinder has become the face of Middle East mayhem's impact on India
description: Global tensions in the Middle East are directly impacting Indian households, causing a severe LPG shortage.  Rising fuel costs and disrupted shipments have led to panic bookings, long queues, and black marketing, forcing businesses to adapt and highlighting India's heavy reliance on imports.  The situation is escalating, affecting daily life and livelihoods across the nation.

title: Tell that ten times: Inside the world of tongue twisters
description: From the seashells she sells on the seashore to Peter Piper’s peppers, tongue twisters have gotten our tongues — and our minds — in a knot. But where did they come from, and why are they so difficult, yet so fun?

title: Sex bias: key to a DNA puzzle
description: Male Neanderthals interbred primarily with female modern humans, a new study has found

title: Cholesterol makes cells’ nuclei squishy, helping melanoma spread
description: When cholesterol levels in the nuclear envelope were high, the nucleus became easier to deform. This is important because cancer cells often have to squeeze through tight gaps between other cells to spread. A squishier nucleus makes that squeezing easier, so the cancer can invade new tissues more successfully

title: The fires of Madras’ kitchens
